themes: Manager-heavy backlog with long wait times: Nearly all contacts are Managers, and a significant portion have been waiting over 90 days — many well past 200 — signaling a systemic gap in proactive outreach cadence that risks churn and dissatisfaction among the practitioners who use SOCi most.

Multi-contact accounts need coordinated engagement: Several accounts (Travel Leaders Group, Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices, Holiday Inn Express, Ford, Anytime Fitness, Avenue5 Residential, etc.) appear multiple times, suggesting fragmented feedback loops and an opportunity to consolidate conversations and deliver a unified response to each brand.

Franchise, multifamily, and travel verticals dominate the queue: The backlog skews heavily toward franchise QSR/fitness/hospitality brands, multifamily property management companies, and travel/cruise networks, pointing to shared pain points around multi-location reporting, local page management at scale, and roll-up visibility that could be addressed with vertical-specific product narratives.
